TEXT.English: [H1]K-Mod to AdvCiv Changes[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Provided by AdvCiv-SAS maintainer/developer, and non-exhaustive list of some info i'd want to display there as well, from AdvCiv's (adjust to your mod path) C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\_0_Common_Docs\AdvCiv_Base_Doc\manual.pdf or similar path for manual.txt or some other AdvCiv-related sources or not.[PARAGRAPH:1]Info below may also not be updated with latest version of the manual.pdf or similar format, view them rather at base AdvCiv's github https://github.com/f1rpo/AdvCiv or some other place that has them or/and is relevant for latest updated content or/and more exhaustive info, this below only provides some key or excerpted info for quick view.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Notable adjustments to K-Mod AI behavior (non-exhaustive as well):[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- AI razes fewer cities and less randomly; 116[NEWLINE]- uses Slavery and Drafting less aggressively, though still frequently; 121b, 017[NEWLINE]- uses destructive espionage missions only against civs it dislikes; 120[NEWLINE]- will rarely found a religion before a second city (reinforcing K-Mod changes that make the AI less interested in the early religions). 171[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Reverted by AdvCiv (i (AdvCiv-SAS maintainer/developer) assume from K-Mod (non-exhaustive as well)):[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- See Immortal Culture about reverted changes to culture and revolts.[NEWLINE]- Production overflow is never invested into an additional unit as this had lead to balance problems with AI civs training multiple Archers per turn. Instead, excess overflow is converted into gold as in the Unofficial Patch, though with some tweaks to make this conversion more difficult to abuse. 064b[NEWLINE]- Gifting Great People to the AI does not provide a relations bonus. 141[NEWLINE]- No impact of global research on inflation; instead, adjusted tech costs based on handicap and the years-per-turn progression for a more historically accurate tech pace, and Immortal and Deity games now start on turn 10. 251, 910[NEWLINE]- Some minor balance changes undone that weren't pulling their weight in terms of added complexity (change 200) (non-exhaustive (AdvCiv-SAS maintainer/develoepr note)): All corporations have the same maintenance multiplier (i.e. mostly as in BtS)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Important miscellaneous changes in AdvCiv (non-exhaustive as well)[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- Workers and settlers only have a 50% chance of being captured and 0% if attacked on the same turn as declaring war. 010[NEWLINE]- When a tile with unfinished worker builds is left alone for more than 7 turns, the invested worker turns begin to decay. This is mainly to disincentivize pre-chopping. 011[NEWLINE]- Forest and Jungle provide only 25% defense, and none if the tile is owned by the attacker. 012[NEWLINE]- Unowned Forests and Jungles can't be chopped down. 119[NEWLINE]- The AI evacuates land units that can't defend well from cities that are about to be attacked and untenable. 139[NEWLINE]- When the AI has more happiness or health than it needs, it may refuse to accept resources in trade or may trade away its only copy of a resource. 036[NEWLINE]- Undefended cities stop "fearing for their safety" once Nationalism is discovered. 500c[NEWLINE]- Slavery base yield reduced to 24 production (from 30). 912d [NEWLINE]- The Spy unit can investigate rival cities, but (unlike in Warlords) with a mission cost. 103[NEWLINE]- Research progress toward a tech makes that tech cheaper to steal. 120i[NEWLINE]- When a citizen starves, the Granary is emptied to delay further starvation. 160[NEWLINE]- The Apostolic Palace can propose war against (non-full) voting members, but only full members are compelled to declare war. kekm.25[NEWLINE]- Can no longer bypass the Theocracy restriction by gifting missionaries. 123a/ kekm.4[NEWLINE]- Damage from nuclear weapons to units made less predictable; SDI interception chance reduced from 75% to 60% and cost increased from 1000 to 1500 production. 650[NEWLINE]- Wonders that have been manually removed from the production queue generate no "fail gold." Also no gold from national units. 123f[NEWLINE]- Trade routes can only come from revealed cities, and the paths to those cities need to be revealed as well. Cities are temporarily unavailable for trade when in disorder. Trade along rivers doesn't require any tech (as in Warlords; BtS demands Sailing). When borders of a war enemy block trade, a naval blockade can override this. Connecting a resource on an island workable by a mainland city requires only a road, not a fort. 124[NEWLINE]- When mountain peaks block off part of a landmass, the parts are treated as separate continents. This is relevant for the placement of Barbarians, colony maintenance and wonders that affect only one continent - though the main point is to make things easier for the AI. 030[NEWLINE]- Land threshold for Domination victory reduced, especially in large games. 254[NEWLINE]- On Emperor handicap and above, some of the AI discounts have been reduced, but human civs face increased production costs, research costs, city growth thresholds and Great Person thresholds. The AI also starts with fewer freebies, in particular, without Agriculture on Immortal and Deity and without a second free Settler on Deity. Progressive AI bonuses are now based on the game turn instead of the AI era. 251/ 250e[NEWLINE]- Marathon speed is only 2.5 times slower than Normal speed, not 3 times. 252[NEWLINE]- The default player count is 8 for the Standard map size. 137[NEWLINE]- Map generation: Clusters of Gold, Gems made less common. Silver may occur on Grassland Forest. No Flood Plains on river corners. Jungle may occur on Plains. 129[NEWLINE]- New algorithm for assigning starting locations. Should partition the available land more evenly than in BtS. Not enabled for all map scripts (list). 027, 108[NEWLINE]- Switched the no-war-probability of Roosevelt and Darius, meaning that Darius can start wars when pleased, and Roosevelt - normally - cannot. 005a[NEWLINE]- Suryavarman's favorite religion is Hinduism and his favorite civic Caste System. 005a[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Other information i scraped/found (long ago (relatively) (at the start of this mod) and can finally add in AdvCiv-SAS):[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:2]About the below info in this section, it comes from/in code comments from base AdvCiv or similar that i have removed our mod (mostly from the old strategy history panel fields we don't use anymore as they are/can be inaccurate/hard to update as well) (non-exhaustive as well); note that these below are not from base AdvCiv's manual so they may be inaccurate even more or not than manual doc added in this page (Mods Info/ Changes from K-Mod to AdvCiv); please also note that we don't update the [LINK=CONCEPT_ADVANCED_START]concepts pages (now in "Outdated" category in AdvCiv-SAS's sevopedia)[\LINK] so part of the info below (at least the one related to concepts, may not apply anymore to our mod at all and is just base AdvCiv info for exhaustiveness, as for all other info we altered some changes made by AdvCiv while we kept many or some, please view docs or/and [LINK=CONCEPT_ADVCIV_SAS_CORE_CHANGES]CONCEPT_ADVCIV_SAS_CORE_CHANGES[\LINK] for details)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]- (AdvCiv): Defense from Walls is not cumulative with defense from city culture. The higher of the two values counts.[NEWLINE]- (AdvCiv): The defense modifier from Castle replaces the one from Wall and is not cumulative with defense from city culture[NEWLINE]- units in friendly cities and forts are safe from Flank Attacks.[NEWLINE]- SISTINE_CHAPEL (note (in) advciv-sas: in AdvCiv-SAS is now replaced with [LINK=BUILDING_NATYA_SHASTRA]Natya Shastra[\LINK] and this is fixed too i.e. it now doubles after x turns like all other buildings too if i am not mistaken) unlike other building [ICON_CULTURE] rates, the added [ICON_CULTURE] rate doesn't double after 1000 years.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_ESPIONAGE Addition: advc.132 allows switching to civics not run by the Spy owner.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_CITIES advc.004f: "We Love the King Day" disabled by AdvCiv.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_LATE_START advc.250b: Added mention of the new SPaH option and a clarification that later-era start and advanced start are separate things. Overwrites text in Civ4GameText_BTS.xml.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_UNITS advc.010: Worker capturing has limitations; advc.005e.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_VASSAL_STATES advc.141: Pointed out change in happiness rule. Removed mention of vassal maintenance "dragging the economy to a halt"; it's not so dramatic. Vassal maint. is still mentioned.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_COMBAT and added info that feature defense depends on tile ownership.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT Added mention of Executives as special cargo.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT advc.123c: Rule change for loading units into cargo mentioned.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_DIPLOMACY Referring reader to the manual about AI refusal to talk (advc.104i) and tribute demands (advc.130o); clarified that the manner of declaring war doesn't make a difference, that tribute can't be demanded from pleased AI leaders, and that the AI doesn't normally declare war when the player declines an offer. Removed mention of "disband[ing] buildings" as this is not possible.[NEWLINE]- TXT_KEY_TRAIT_CREATIVE_PEDIA (note: advc.908b if i am not mistaken from my notes of these?) Since K-Mod and AdvCiv make [ICON_CULTURE] points more worthwhile in several ways, AdvCiv reduces the [ICON_CULTURE] rate (was 2 in BtS) and adds some free initial [ICON_CULTURE] instead. AdvCiv has also removed the faster [ICON_PRODUCTION] of Colosseum - which doesn't seem to fit the culture theme well. (note: advc.908b if i am not mistaken from my notes of these?) [NEWLINE]- _CIVIC_STATE_PROPERTY_ K-Mod treats colony maintenance as separate from distance maintenance, so this civic no longer affects colony maintenance.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_PROMOTIONS Overwrites CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts. Just correcting a bit of misinformation: When it comes to combat XP, it does matter who attacked.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_RESOURCES Overwrites CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts. Correction: Duplicate resources do have uses with BtS.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_COMBAT advc.012: Corrected two examples that say that Forest grants 50% def, Weirdly, an example in TXT_KEY_CONCEPT_TERRAIN_PEDIA already said that Forest defense is 25%. Perhaps it was changed to 50% late in development.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_ESPIONAGE [advc.103] Added note about Spies being able to investigate cities.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_FOOD [advc.160] Replaced "half" of the food with "a portion". Added mention of the Granary under "Insufficient Food".[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT advc.906: Submarine becomes Attack Submarine (note: i also added this note if i am mistaken and does not come from base AdvCiv, adding it for exhaustiveness and just in case as well......: advc.005e (but did not check 005e though))[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_UNITED_NATIONS advc.148: Overwriting the key in CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts.xml. AI voting behavior outlined. Victory vote was missing entirely in German.[NEWLINE]- CIV_INCA advc.004g: Dropped mention of Quechua

[H1]AdvCiv to AdvCiv-SAS Core Changes[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Non-exhaustive list of changes in AdvCiv-SAS as compared to AdvCiv (excluding most balance changes, for that please refer to the Sevopedia entries of the items you want to see, for example "Units", "Technologies", or "Traits" to have the latest updated version of their effects.)[PARAGRAPH:2]For more details, in particular about the changes, please also visit:[NEWLINE]- if online, https://github.com/wonderingabout/AdvCiv-SAS/, in particular the "Main Changes Guide" there (also appended/included as a text .md file in AdvCiv-SAS). If you want to see the full very exhaustive code changes between AdvCiv current latest stable, for example 1.12 here, and AdvCiv-SAS, please visit this pull request compare: https://github.com/wonderingabout/AdvCiv-SAS/pull/13 . Be warned though it can be very lengthy, so read rather the Main Changes Guide mentionned above preferably for the shorter and less exhaustive version if you'd prefer.[NEWLINE]- if offline: you can also view most (almost all) of the information above by navigating the docs in your AdvCiv-SAS folder (for example if you have the Steam version of the game,it would be here (else adapt to where your civ4 and advciv-sas install paths are): "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\"), and opening them with a text editor (i recommend Notepad++ (or (even) better depending on the need VS Code .md preview for example), but the default Windows Notepad.exe can also open .txt and .md files too just with less display features (but still all info unless i'm mistaken anyways)).

[H1]About the AI Personality Panel in AdvCiv-SAS[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]An exhaustive/extensive documentation has been written about the AI Personality Panel feature (that is) in AdvCiv-SAS (in the SevopediaLeader category), explaining, in broad, general ways how it works and how to read the data from a new player's perspective.[PARAGRAPH:2]It is recommended to read it to understand better how it works.[PARAGRAPH:2]You can view it:[NEWLINE]- online on github (todo add link),[NEWLINE]- or even if you are offline, you can read the .md files as any text file, for example with Notepad++ (or the default Notepad.exe of windows too if you prefer but Notepad.exe has less advanced features, still more than good enough for reading a doc especially if you prefer and should display all info as well if i'm not mistaken anyways). The file path is, for example if you have the Steam version of Civ4 (adapt to where your game and mod is if you use a non-Steam (or other than Steam) version (maybe rather anyways): "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\README_AI_Personality_Panel.md")[PARAGRAPH:2]You'll get most info from there hopefully rather than me copy pasting the same doc (painfully hehe but that i would gladly do (maybe or not or other etc anyways) but better avoid if possible anyways) in sevopedia ingame and it being potentially outdated after not long, etc anyways, thanks.

[H1]Rivers[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Rivers are exceptional terrain in that they are not "in" or "on" a tile, but they run between tiles. A river is considered adjacent to all tiles that it runs along, including all four tiles which touch a place where the river bends or empties into water. However, the two tiles which touch the source of a river and are behind it, with respect to the river's flow, are not riverside. Rivers supply fresh water to all adjacent tiles. A river adds +1 [ICON_COMMERCE] to all adjacent land tiles, unless they also contain an unimproved forest or jungle. Only riverside Cities may build [LINK=BUILDING_LEVEE]Levees[\LINK], which provide +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] to every riverside tile within that City's radius.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Terrain_(Civ4)#Rivers

[H1]Route (Road)[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Roads and railroads allow units to move longer distances within a turn by reducing the movement costs of terrain.[PARAGRAPH:1]When a unit is within hostile cultural borders, roads and railroads have no effect unless the unit has the Commando promotion. When the civilization has an Open Borders agreement allowing their units right of passage, they may freely take advantage of roads and railroads.[PARAGRAPH:2]Roads can be built after discovering The Wheel. With roads, all spaces cost 1/2 [ICON_MOVES] Movement points (MP) to enter from other road tiles. After discovering [LINK=TECH_ENGINEERING]Engineering[\LINK], the cost goes down to 1/3 [ICON_MOVES] MP. In practice, this means that a unit with just one movement point can move three tiles using roads. Similarly, units with two movement points can move six tiles.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Road_and_railroad_(Civ4)

[H1]Route (Railroad)[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Roads and railroads allow units to move longer distances within a turn by reducing the movement costs of terrain.[PARAGRAPH:1]When a unit is within hostile cultural borders, roads and railroads have no effect unless the unit has the Commando promotion. When the civilization has an Open Borders agreement allowing their units right of passage, they may freely take advantage of roads and railroads.[PARAGRAPH:2]To build railroads, the civilization first needs to discover the Railroad technology and have an access to Coal or Oil. Railroads are similar to roads, but they reduce the number of [ICON_MOVES] Movement points needed to enter a tile from another railroad tile to 1/10 [ICON_MOVES] MP.[PARAGRAPH:1]Railroads also give a bonus of 1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] to tiles with a [LINK=IMPROVEMENT_MINE]Mine[\LINK], a [LINK=IMPROVEMENT_LUMBERMILL]Lumbermill[\LINK], or a [LINK=IMPROVEMENT_QUARRY]Quarry[\LINK].[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Road_and_railroad_(Civ4)

[H1]Global Warming[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Global warming is a game element in Civilization IV: Beyond the Sword.[NEWLINE][H2]Overview[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]Global warming is caused by pollution (namely, "unhealthy" buildings) and nuclear explosions (which may sound counter-intuitive but is seemingly normal in the Civilization series). It is adverted by a large number of forests and jungles present on the world map. Its effects are to reduce the production of tiles, usually turning random tiles into desert. The tiles affected when global warming strikes are picked randomly, regardless of how much each nation contributed to its occurrence.[NEWLINE][H2]Mechanics[\H2][NEWLINE]Effect of Global Warming[PARAGRAPH:1]If Global Warming strikes, a random land PLOT which is not a city will be chosen.[PARAGRAPH:1]There is no regional, player, team or other bias in choosing the target plot for Global Warming. It does not matter who, where or why caused the nuclear explosions, built the polluting buildings, chopped or preserved the forests.[PARAGRAPH:1]If PLOT has a feature (Forest, Jungle, Floodplain, Oasis), this feature will be removed, but the terrain type does not change (unless the feature is Fallout, in which case nothing happens at all).[PARAGRAPH:1]If PLOT has no Feature and can give any yield (i.e. is not desert or frozen), then it will be turned into GLOBAL_WARMING_TERRAIN, which is - per standard XML - desert.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Note on probability[PARAGRAPH:1]This de facto reduces the Probability of Global Warming with increasing amount of useless land plots on the map. Since if a plot is chosen to be victim of Global Warming, that can not be worked anyway, nothing happens. So actually:[PARAGRAPH:1]PROBGW_strikes = 1 - (1 - PROB1 + PROB1*#BADLAND/#LAND)GW_VALUE[PARAGRAPH:1]Depending on how high PROB1 actually is and how much "bad land" we have it might result in 5-10 % (or even more in really extreme cases) less visible GW.[PARAGRAPH:1]Same thing with fallout. Should be negligible in most games, but could become interesting in a huge Nuclear War, where the Fallout will actually help preserve the terrain under it. Of course, as soon as you clear the Fallout after the war, the plots will become affected by Global Warming again.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Notes[PARAGRAPH:1]Nukes now count only half. This means that a single explosion (say a meltdown) will not cause GW by itself. However this is only true if there are no other sources of GW.[PARAGRAPH:1]The [ICON_UNHEALTHY] from buildings is weighted by map size (number of plots).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source with more details: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Global_warming_(Civ4)

[H1]Customization[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Civilization IV is much more open to modification than its predecessors. Game data and rules are stored in XML files, and a Software Development Kit was released in April 2006 to allow AI customization. Major parts of the interface, map generation, and scripted events are written entirely in Python and can be customised.[NEWLINE][H2]World Builder[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The World Builder allows a player to create a map from scratch, to use an in-game situation as a starting point for a new scenario, or to simply cheat by giving himself more units, gold, buildings, better relations with leaders, improvements, cultural borders, technologies, even take over other cities by placing a border there. The terrain can be altered, and resources, military units and cities on the map can be added, removed or modified. Additionally, each civilization's technological progress as well as its diplomatic and military ties to other civilizations can be edited. The World Builder for Civ IV is in-game, in contrast to previous Civilization games where the Map Editor was an external application. (In Civ2 one could modify anything using the cheat menu.)[PARAGRAPH:1]World Builder can also be used to create an interesting experience. In custom game mode, the user must first turn all opposing civilizations off as well as all victory conditions. Barbarians must also be deactivated. In World Builder, the user can make it so that the player only starts with a settler with no starting technologies. This allows the user to progress through all technologies for a more expanded experience of the development of civilization.[NEWLINE][H2]XML[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]More game attributes are stored in XML files, which must be edited with an external text editor or application. Barry Caudill, a senior producer at Firaxis Games, said [1] in September:[PARAGRAPH:1]"Editing these files will allow players to tweak simple game rules and change or add content. For instance, they can add new unit or building types, change the cost of wonders, or add new civilizations. Players can also change the sounds played at certain times or edit the play list for your soundtrack."[PARAGRAPH:1]At the current time the XML processing in Windows is more permissive of minor errors than in Mac OS X. As a result, some XML files which will work on the Windows version of the game need some slight tweaking before they function on the Macintosh version.[NEWLINE][H2]Python[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The game uses boost.python to allow the Python programming language access to many parts of the game (including the style and content of all interface screens). Python can also be used to modify random map generation and to add complex scripted events.[PARAGRAPH:1]The version of Python present in the Windows version of the game differs from the version in Mac OS X up to and including version 10.4.7, and as a result, while most Python files for the Windows version will work on the Macintosh version, not all will.[NEWLINE][H2]Software Development Kit[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The Civilization IV Software Development Kit was released on April 13 2006 to coincide with the release of the v1.61 patch. The kit allows players to view, modify, or completely re-write the game's DLL source code, enabling the modification of the game's AI and other integral parts of the game.[PARAGRAPH:1]As of the first official patch for the Macintosh version (v1.61 Revision A), there is no SDK for the Macintosh version of the game. In fact the Macintosh version lacks the separate library of game related code which the PC version uses, but instead includes the code compiled into the main executable. There is as of yet no indication of whether this will change in a future patch.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Civilization_IV#Customization

[H1]Fresh Water[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Any tile adjacent to a river, lake, or oasis has fresh water. The following rules apply to tiles with fresh water:[PARAGRAPH:1][ICON_BULLET]Any city built on a fresh water tile gains a permanent bonus of +2 [ICON_HEALTHY].[PARAGRAPH:1][ICON_BULLET][LINK=IMPROVEMENT_FARM]Farms[\LINK] require fresh water for [LINK=CONCEPT_IRRIGATION]irrigation[\LINK].[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Fresh_water_(Civ4)

[H1]Irrigation[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2][LINK=IMPROVEMENT_FARM]Farms[\LINK] require irrigation in order to provide their full [LINK=CONCEPT_FOOD]food[\LINK] benefits. Irrigation can be achieved by building the farm adjacent to a source of [LINK=CONCEPT_FRESH_WATER]fresh water[\LINK], like a river, lake, or oasis.[PARAGRAPH:2][LINK=TECH_CIVIL_SERVICE]Civil Service[\LINK] enables irrigated farms to become a source of irrigation themselves. That allows you to bring irrigation to distant locations by building a chain of farms from the source of fresh water.[PARAGRAPH:2]With the advent of [LINK=TECH_BIOLOGY]Biology[\LINK], Farms no longer require irrigation.

[H1]Scoring System[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]In Civilization IV the leadership skills of players are compared to a subjective list of twenty of the best or worst leaders in history, similar to the list in Civilization I. The score is based on a number of factors, including military growth and success, technological advancement, construction of wonders and economic growth. It is shown at the end of the game.[PARAGRAPH:2]The released version of the game abandoned Civilization III's graded scale. In Civ3, a spectacular victory on the easiest handicap would provide the player with only a middling score, and the best titles were only awarded to players attempting the hardest difficulties. The original Civ4, on the other hand, allows the player to obtain any score on any handicap level. As of the v1.61 patch, the grading system has returned to the curved-by-handicap scale.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source with more info: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Civilization_IV#Scoring_system
